title: Archive Show
description: When reviewing archived work, users need to inspect a specific change without restoring it. The `specd archive show <name>` command displays basic metadata for a single archived change.
context: When reviewing archived work, users need to inspect a specific change without restoring it. The `specd archive show <name>` command displays basic metadata for a single archived change.

rules:
```
specd archive show <name> [--format text|json|toon]
```

- `<name>` — required positional; the name of the archived change to show
- `--format` — controls output encoding; defaults to `text`

The command prints read-only details for the archived change to stdout:

```
name:        <name>
state:       <lifecycle-state>
archivedAt:  <iso-timestamp>
archivedBy:  <name <email>>        # omitted when not recorded
specs:       <specId>, ...
schema:      <schema-name>@<version>
artifacts:   <artifactType>, ...   # artifact type IDs from the archive record
```

The `state` field MUST be derived from the archived `manifest.json` lifecycle state at load time. It MUST NOT be hardcoded to `archivable`.

When `--format json` is passed, the command writes a single JSON object to stdout:

```json
{
  \"name\":\"...\",
  \"state\":\"...\",
  \"archivedAt\":\"...\",
  \"archivedBy\":{ \"name\":\"...\", \"email\":\"...\" },
  \"specIds\":[...],
  \"schema\":{ \"name\":\"...\", \"version\":N },
  \"artifacts\":[\"...\"]
}
```

`archivedBy` is omitted when not recorded. `artifacts` is an array of artifact type IDs present at archive time.
When `--format toon` is passed, the command writes the same data model as JSON encoded in Token-Oriented Object Notation (toon) to stdout.
- If no change with the given name exists in the archive directory, the command exits with code 1 and prints an `error:` message to stderr.

constraints: Errors always go to stderr as plain text regardless of --format

scenarios.name: Missing name argument
when: specd archive show is run without a name
then: the command exits with code 1 and prints a usage error to stderr

scenarios.name: TOON format output
when: specd archive show my-change --format toon is run
then: output is encoded in Token-Oriented Object Notation (toon)

scenarios.name: Normal text output
given: the archive directory contains a change add-oauth-login archived with lifecycle state archivable, specIds ['auth:oauth'], and schema schema-std@1
when: specd archive show add-oauth-login is run
then: stdout contains name:, state:, archivedAt:, specs:, schema:, and artifacts: fields with the correct values
AND the state: line reflects the archived manifest lifecycle state (not a hardcoded value)
AND the process exits with code 0

scenarios.name: JSON format output
given: the archive directory contains a change add-oauth-login with lifecycle state archivable, specIds ['auth:oauth'], and schema schema-std@1
when: specd archive show add-oauth-login --format json is run
then: stdout is a JSON object with name, state, archivedAt, specIds, schema, and artifacts fields matching the change
AND the state field reflects the archived manifest lifecycle state
AND the process exits with code 0

scenarios.name: Change not found in archive
when: specd archive show nonexistent is run and no change named nonexistent exists in the archive
then: AND stderr contains an error: message
